paper_title: Measurement of Development Characteristics of Pistillate Flowers and Fruit Cluster

methods.description: This method involves sampling and dissecting pistillate inflorescences to record the number and percentage of pistillate flowers per inflorescence during the peak flowering period.

steps.description: In both 2012 and 2013, more than 300 pistillate inflorescences from each of five germplasms were randomly sampled during the peak flowering period (mid-April).
steps.searchable_summary: randomly sample pistillate inflorescences during peak flowering

steps.description: Every pistillate inflorescence was dissected with a scalpel and forceps, and the number of pistillate flowers per inflorescence and the percentage of pistillate flowers per inflorescence were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: dissect inflorescences and record flower counts and percentages

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting pollen, performing artificial pollination, and monitoring pistillate inflorescences and fruit clusters for abscission and fruit set.

steps.description: On April 1st of both years, pollen was collected from the hazel orchards and air-dried using the method described by Liu et al. (2012).
steps.searchable_summary: collect and air-dry pollen from hazel orchards

steps.description: After mixing equal amounts of pollen from each individual and pollen germination test on stigma of cultivar Dawei, artificial pollination was carried out in the peak flowering period (mid-April).
steps.searchable_summary: mix pollen and perform artificial pollination

steps.description: Results of pollen germination test indicated that more than 75% pollen grains could germinate, while the germination ratio of fresh mixed pollen on fresh stigma of cultivar Dawei was about 86%.

Twelve trees consistent in height and growth were selected for each hybrid hazel cultivar and C. heterophylla, and more than 300 pistillate inflorescences of each individual were artificially pollinated with brush and marked with tags.
steps.searchable_summary: select trees and perform artificial pollination with tagging

steps.description: Concurrently, more than 300 open-pollinated pistillate inflorescences were randomly selected and marked with tags (the control).
steps.searchable_summary: select and tag open-pollinated inflorescences as control

steps.description: The tagged pistillate inflorescences and fruit clusters were monitored every week to determine the frequency of abscission from pollination to fruit harvest.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor tagged inflorescences and fruit clusters for abscission

steps.description: On August 20th of both years, all remaining and tagged fruit clusters were harvested.
Average number of fruit (ANF) per cluster, average number of abortive ovary (ANAO) per cluster, and percentage of abortive ovaries (PAO) per cluster were calculated.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate ANF, ANAO, and PAO per cluster

steps.description: Fruit set as percentage of total number of pistillate flowers per plant (FS) was calculated using the following formula: FS = 100% × (fruit number after pistillate inflorescences tagged)/(number of tagged pistillate inflorescences × number of pistillate flowers per inflorescence).
After dissection of the fresh nut, the shell mass, kernel mass and total nut mass were measured without drying.
steps.searchable_summary: measure shell, kernel, and total nut mass

steps.description: The kernel ratio was calculated with the following formula: kernel ratio = kernel mass × 100%/total nut mass.

methods.description: This method describes the collection and preparation of female inflorescences and young fruits for histological examination or fluorescence microscopy observation.

steps.description: Female inflorescences and young fruits were sampled every 1-5 days from mid-April to late August in 2012 and 2013.
steps.searchable_summary: sample female inflorescences and young fruits periodically

steps.description: The samples destined for histological examination or fluorescence microscopy observation were fixed in FAA solution (70% ethanol: glacial acetic acid: formalin, 18:1:1 [v/v/v]) for 3 days, then stored in 70% alcohol at 4 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: fix samples in FAA solution and store in alcohol
